name: Corn, Coconut and Fennel Chowder
recipeIngredient: 2 cups leeks, stems removed - cut into thin rings
1 teaspoon fresh serrano chiles - seeded and slivered
4 cups fresh corn kernels - cut from the cob with cobs reserved
1 tablespoon finely slivered fresh lime or lemon zest
Fresh cilantro or mint leaves - finely diced
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onions and leeks; sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
2. Stir in garlic, fennel seed, red pepper flakes, and serrano chiles. Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
3. In a separate pot, bring chicken stock to a simmer with the reserved corn cobs. Simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain through a fine-mesh sieve into the first pot.
4. Dissolve cornstarch in coconut milk and whisk into the broth. Add lime zest and simmer gently until thickened, about 5 minutes.
5. Stir in corn kernels, fennel, and salt/pepper to taste. Warm through for another minute.
6. Remove from heat and stir in cilantro just before serving. Adjust thickness with additional stock if desired.
7. Garnish with fresh cilantro or mint leaves and finely diced red bell peppers. Serve hot.
Yield: 6 to 8 servings
